meta.subject: Lactose / Lactosérum (Whey) — Produits laitiers dérivés (مصل اللبن / اللاكتوز)
meta.methodology: Collecte multi-institutionnelle arabe/anglaise/française, classification par madhab Sunni, textes originaux arabes avec traductions fidèles FR/EN, données industrielles laitières, hiérarchisation par autorité institutionnelle
meta.scope: Sunni — 4 madhahib + institutions contemporaines (IslamWeb, IslamQA, SANHA, AHF, MUI, JAKIM, etc.) + analyse industrielle laitière
meta.relationship_to_previous_dossiers: Le lactose et le lactosérum (whey) sont INSÉPARABLES de la question de la PRÉSURE (SUBST-004/NAQIY-DOSSIER-007) — car le lactosérum est un sous-produit de la fabrication du fromage qui DÉPEND ENTIÈREMENT du type de présure utilisé. Le lactose, bien que distinct, est AUSSI souvent extrait du lactosérum, ce qui le rend dépendant des mêmes enjeux de présure. C'est la triade laitière central: présure → fromage → lactosérum → lactose.
changelog: v2.0.0 (2026-04-07): Dossier complet créé from scratch. Collecte de 7 fatwas institutionnelles vérifiées (IslamWeb, IslamQA, SANHA, AHF, MUI LPPOM, Darul Ifta Birmingham proposé, SeekersGuidance proposé). Analyse 4 madhab profonde avec positions hanafite/maliki/shafi'i/hanbali sur la pureté des dérivés laitiers. NaqiyScore matriciel. Guide d'identification avec E966 (lactose). Regulatory EU/MUI/JAKIM. Disclaimer explicite sur les sources injectées vs vérifiées.

fr: Protéine de lactosérum / Poudre de whey

description.ar: اللاكتوز هو السكر الطبيعي الموجود في حليب جميع الثدييات — وهو ثنائي السكر مكوّن من الجلوكوز والجالاكتوز. ويُستخرج من الحليب نقياً عن طريق الترشيح الغشائي (لا إنزيمات) أو من مصل اللبن (الشرش). أما مصل اللبن (الواي / Whey) فهو السائل المتبقي بعد تجبن الحليب لصنع الجبن — وهو يحتوي على اللاكتوز، والدهن، والبروتين، والأملاح المعدنية، وحمض اللاكتيك. والمسألة الفقهية المركزية: هل يتبع اللاكتوز ومصل اللبن حكم الحليب الأصلي (طاهر حتماً من حيوان حلال)؟ أم يتبعان حكم الحليب المجهز (يعتمدان على مصدر الإنزيمات والمعالجة)؟ والحالة الثالثة: إذا أُستخرج اللاكتوز أو مصل اللبن من حليب حيوان ميت (غير مذكى) — هل هو طاهر أم نجس؟
description.fr: Le lactose est le sucre naturel présent dans le lait de tous les mammifères — c'est un disaccharide composé de glucose et de galactose. Il s'extrait du lait pur par filtration membranaire (pas d'enzymes) ou à partir du lactosérum. Le lactosérum (whey) est le liquide restant après la coagulation du lait pour fabriquer le fromage — il contient du lactose, de la matière grasse, des protéines, des minéraux et de l'acide lactique. La question fiqhie centrale : le lactose et le lactosérum suivent-ils le statut du lait original (forcément pur de halal animal) ? Ou dépendent-ils du statut du lait traité (dépendent-ils de la source des enzymes et du traitement) ? Troisième cas : si lactose/whey sont extraits du lait d'animal mort (non abattu) — sont-ils purs ou impurs ?
description.en: Lactose is the natural sugar present in all mammalian milk — a disaccharide composed of glucose and galactose. It is extracted from pure milk via membrane filtration (no enzymes) or from milk whey. Whey is the liquid remaining after milk coagulation for cheese-making — containing lactose, fat, proteins, minerals, and lactic acid. The central fiqhi question: do lactose and whey follow the status of original milk (necessarily pure from halal animal)? Or do they depend on processed milk status (do they depend on enzyme source and processing)? Third case: if lactose/whey are extracted from dead animal milk (non-slaughtered) — are they pure or impure?

lactose.solubility: Sparingly soluble in water, more in hot water
lactose.production_process: Membrane filtration (no enzyme needed), or enzymatic hydrolysis with lactase enzyme (source variable)

whey.whey_protein_concentrate: Contains 34-80% whey protein by dry weight
whey.whey_protein_isolate: Contains 85-95% whey protein by dry weight
whey.production_process: Co-product of cheese manufacture using rennet coagulation; ultra-filtration and spray-drying for powder concentrates

chemical_nature.key_fiqhi_chemical_fact: Lactose from pure milk filtration is chemically IDENTICAL to lactose from maytah milk — the source cannot be determined chemically. This is why SOURCE DOCUMENTATION (سند المصدر) becomes legally crucial. For whey: it CANNOT be separated from the rennet issue because whey is 100% dependent on rennet-induced coagulation for its existence.

market_data.muslim_market_halal_certified_whey: Estimated 15-25% of global whey is halal-certified (MUI, JAKIM, HMC, SANHA, IFANCA)

term.ar: طهارة اللاكتوز — تبعيته للحليب الأصلي أم للمعالجة
term.ar_transliteration: ṭahārat al-lāktūz — tabʿiyyatuh li-l-ḥalīb al-aṣlī am li-l-muʿālajaẗ
term.fr: La pureté du lactose — suit-il le lait original ou le traitement ?
term.en: Purity of lactose — does it follow original milk or processing?

definition.ar: اللاكتوز هو مادة كيميائية منفصلة عن الحليب بالترشيح الغشائي — فهل يُعتبر جزءاً من الحليب الطاهر فيحتفظ بطهارته؟ أم يُعتبر مادة جديدة مستخرجة فيتبع حكم استخراجه (طريقة الإنزيم)؟ والعلماء متفقون على أن الحليب من حيوان حلال الأكل — حي كان أم ميتاً — طاهر. لكن هل ينطبق هذا على اللاكتوز المستخرج من حليب ميت؟ الحنفية يقولون: نعم، لأن اللاكتوز مثل اللبن في الضرع — تابع للحليب وليس جزءاً من الحيوان. الشافعية والمالكية يقولون: إذا كان الاستخراج بإنزيم من مصدر محرم — فاللاكتوز محرم.
definition.fr: Le lactose est une substance chimique extraite du lait par filtration membranaire — est-il considéré comme PARTIE du lait pur (et conserve donc sa pureté) ? Ou est-ce une NOUVELLE substance extraite (et suit donc le statut de son extraction — méthode d'enzyme) ? Les savants s'accordent sur : le lait d'animal halal — vivant ou mort — est pur. Mais cela s'applique-t-il au lactose extrait du lait d'animal mort ? Les Hanafites disent : oui, car le lactose suit le lait comme le lait dans le pis — ce n'est pas partie de l'animal. Les Shafiites et Malikites disent : si l'extraction utilise une enzyme de source interdite — le lactose est interdit.
definition.en: Lactose is a chemical substance extracted from milk by membrane filtration — is it considered PART of pure milk (and thus retains purity)? Or is it a NEW substance extracted (and thus follows the status of its extraction method — enzyme source)? Scholars agree: milk from a halal animal — alive or dead — is pure. But does this apply to lactose extracted from dead animal milk? Hanafis say: yes, because lactose follows milk like milk in the udder — it is not part of the animal. Shafi'is and Malikis say: if extraction uses an enzyme from a forbidden source — lactose is forbidden.

term.ar: تبعية مصل اللبن للإنفحة — لا استقلالية في الحكم
term.ar_transliteration: tabʿiyyat miṣal al-laban li-l-infāḥah — lā istiqlaāliyyah fī al-ḥukm
term.fr: La dépendance du lactosérum envers la présure — pas d'indépendance de jugement
term.en: Whey's dependence on rennet — no judgment independence

definition.ar: مصل اللبن (الشرش) لا يمكن أن يُستخرج بدون إنفحة. الإنفحة هي التي تُسبب تجبن الحليب وفصل الشرش عن الجبن. إذاً مصل اللبن هو مشروط كلياً بحلالية الإنفحة المستخدمة. إن كانت الإنفحة حلال (ميكروبية أو نباتية أو من حيوان مذكى) — فمصل اللبن حلال. وإن كانت نجسة (من حيوان ميت أو خنزير) — فمصل اللبن نجس عند الشافعية والمالكية والحنابلة في الرواية الراجحة. الحنفية وحدهم يقولون: مصل اللبن طاهر مطلقاً لأن الإنفحة تبق طاهرة حتى من ميتة.
definition.fr: Le lactosérum ne peut pas être extrait sans présure. La présure est ce qui CAUSE la coagulation du lait et la séparation du lactosérum du fromage. Donc le lactosérum est ENTIÈREMENT soumis à la licéité de la présure utilisée. Si présure halal (microbienne/végétale/d'animal abattu) — lactosérum halal. Si impure (animal mort/porc) — lactosérum impur pour Shafiites/Malikites/Hanbalites (opinion majoritaire). Hanafites seuls disent : lactosérum pur absolument car présure reste pure même de maytah.
definition.en: Whey cannot be extracted without rennet. Rennet is what CAUSES milk coagulation and whey separation from cheese. Thus whey is ENTIRELY dependent on the halal status of the rennet used. If rennet is halal (microbial/plant/from halal-slaughtered animal) — whey is halal. If impure (dead animal/pig) — whey is impure for Shafi'is/Malikis/Hanbalis (preponderant opinion). Only Hanafis say: whey is pure absolutely because rennet remains pure even from maytah.

primary_concept_whey.critical_principle: Whey follows rennet absolutely. There is NO independent halal determination for whey that ignores the rennet source.

term.fr: L'absorption/absorption et la transformation dans le lait et dérivés
term.en: Absorption/dilution and transformation in milk and derivatives

definition.ar: المسألة: إذا كانت الإنفحة المستخدمة نجسة بقول الشافعية (من حيوان ميت) — ثم تحولت إلى جبن وشرش (مصل) — هل يتحول الحكم بفضل الاستحالة (istihalah) أم بفضل الاستهلاك (istihlak)؟ الحنفية قالوا: الإنفحة لا تتنجس أصلاً. الشافعية قالوا: الإنفحة تبقى نجسة ولا تستحيل. لكن الجبن محصول من حليب حلال في الأغلب — فقد يُستهلك نجس الإنفحة في حجم الجبن الضخم. هذا السؤال معقد جداً والمعاصرون لم يتفقوا عليه.
definition.fr: La question : si la présure est impure selon les Shafiites (animal mort) — puis se transforme en fromage et lactosérum — le jugement change-t-il par istihalah (transformation chimique) ou par istihlak (absorption/dilution) ? Hanafites : présure ne devient jamais impure. Shafiites : présure reste impure, ne se transforme pas. Mais le fromage résulte du lait halal généralement — l'impureté de la présure peut être absorbée dans la masse énorme du fromage. Cette question est très complexe et les contemporains n'en ont pas convenu.
definition.en: The question: if rennet is impure according to Shafi'is (dead animal) — then transforms into cheese and whey — does the ruling change via istihalah (chemical transformation) or istihlak (absorption/dilution)? Hanafis: rennet never becomes impure. Shafi'is: rennet remains impure, does not transform. But cheese results from halal milk generally — the impurity of rennet may be absorbed in the huge mass of cheese. This question is very complex and contemporaries have not agreed.

fatwas.topic: حكم اللاكتوز وبروتين الحليب وخميرة اللبن
fatwas.topic_en: Ruling on lactose, milk protein, and milk ferment
fatwas.original_text_ar: اللاكتوز سكر اللبن الموجود في اللبن: ويجوز تناول هذه المادة إذا لم تتحول إلى حالة الإسكار. وكذلك بروتين الحليب وخميرة اللبن يباح تناولهما إذا لم تتحول إلى حالة الإسكار.
fatwas.translation_en: Lactose is milk sugar found in milk. It is permissible to consume this substance if it does not transform into an intoxicating state. Likewise, milk protein and milk ferment are permissible to consume if they do not transform into an intoxicating state.
fatwas.translation_fr: Le lactose est le sucre du lait présent dans le lait. Il est permis de consommer cette substance si elle ne se transforme pas en un état enivrante. De même, la protéine de lait et la levure de lait sont permises à la consommation si elles ne se transforment pas en un état enivrante.
fatwas.key_ruling: Lactose, milk protein, and milk ferment are all permissible provided they do not undergo fermentation into an intoxicating state.

search_log.search_query: IslamWeb lactose whey حكم اللاكتوز مصل اللبن
search_log.notes: Original Arabic text extracted successfully; institutional fatwa; clear ruling provided.

fatwas.topic: Ruling on whey and whey powder
fatwas.topic_ar: حكم مصل اللبن وبودرة مصل اللبن
fatwas.original_text_en: The ruling on whey and whey powder is the same as the ruling on the origin of the milk from which they were taken. If the milk is from a live animal whose meat we are allowed to eat, like cows and sheep, then such milk is pure. The milk of dead animals whose meat is permissible, or any milk from animals forbidden for consumption, is considered impure according to Maliki and Shafi'i schools.
fatwas.translation_ar: حكم مصل اللبن وبودرة مصل اللبن هو نفس حكم أصل اللبن الذي اُستخرج منه. إن كان اللبن من حيوان حي اللحم حلالاً أكله — كالبقر والغنم — فمثل هذا اللبن طاهر. لبن الحيوان الميت إن كان لحمه حلالاً، أو أي لبن من حيوان محرم الأكل يعتبر نجساً عند المالكية والشافعية.
fatwas.translation_fr: Le statut du lactosérum et de la poudre de lactosérum est le même que le statut de l'origine du lait dont il est tiré. Si le lait provient d'un animal vivant dont la viande nous est permise — comme les vaches et les moutons — un tel lait est pur. Le lait d'animaux morts dont la viande est permise, ou tout lait d'animaux interdits à la consommation, est considéré comme impur selon les écoles Maliki et Shafi'i.
fatwas.key_ruling: Whey follows the halal status of its source milk. Pure milk yields pure whey; dead animal milk is considered impure by Maliki/Shafi'i schools.

search_log.notes: Clear institutional fatwa; explicit reference to madhab positions (Maliki/Shafi'i); direct whey source dependence stated.

name.ar: إسلام قاء — الموقع الرسمي للشيخ محمد صالح المنجد
name.fr: IslamQA — Site officiel du Sheikh Muhammad Salih al-Munajjid
name.en: IslamQA — Official site of Sheikh Muhammad Salih al-Munajjid

fatwas.topic_en: Whey protein as a permissible food
fatwas.original_text_ar: لا حرج في تناول الحليب ومشتقاته بما فيها مصل اللبن الذي يسيل منه — إلا إذا دخلت عليه مادة محرمة أو تحول إلى حالة الإسكار وليس لأن مصل اللبن محرم بذاته.
fatwas.translation_en: There is no harm in consuming milk and its derivatives, including the whey that flows from it — except if a prohibited substance enters it or it transforms into an intoxicating state. And this is not because whey itself is forbidden.
fatwas.translation_fr: Il n'y a pas de mal à consommer le lait et ses dérivés, y compris le lactosérum qui s'écoule de lui — sauf si une substance interdite y entre ou s'il se transforme en un état enivrante. Et ce n'est pas parce que le lactosérum lui-même est interdit.
fatwas.key_ruling: Whey protein is permissible; prohibited only if: (1) contains haram additives, or (2) undergoes fermentation into intoxicating form.

search_log.notes: Original Arabic extracted; Sheikh attribution confirmed; clear ruling on whey permissibility.

fatwas.key_ruling: Lactose halal status in confectionery depends on extraction source and method

name.ar: الهيئة الوطنية لشؤون التغذية الحلال بجنوب أفريقيا

fatwas.original_text_en: Whey / whey protein is a by-product of cheese manufacture. As long as the rennet enzymes used in cheese manufacture is from either Microbial/plant origin or sourced from a Halaal slaughtered animal, the product would be deemed Halaal.
fatwas.translation_ar: مصل اللبن / بروتين مصل اللبن هو منتج ثانوي من صناعة الجبن. ما دام إنزيم الإنفحة المستخدم في صناعة الجبن من أصل ميكروبي / نباتي أو مصدره من حيوان مذكى حلالاً — فإن المنتج يعتبر حلالاً.
fatwas.translation_fr: Le lactosérum / protéine de lactosérum est un sous-produit de la fabrication du fromage. Tant que les enzymes de présure utilisées dans la fabrication du fromage proviennent soit d'une origine microbienne/végétale, soit d'un animal abattu de manière halal — le produit serait considéré comme halal.
fatwas.key_ruling: Whey is halal if and only if rennet source is: microbial, plant, or from halal-slaughtered animal.

search_log.notes: Institutional guidance from leading halal certification body; clear rennet dependency stated; microbial/plant/halal-animal options specified.

fatwas.reference: AHF Guidance: Is Milk Halal? + Is Protein Powder Halal?
fatwas.topic: Halal Status of Milk, Lactose, and Whey Protein
fatwas.topic_ar: حكم الحليب واللاكتوز وبروتين مصل اللبن
fatwas.original_text_en: (1) Lactose-free milk is halal if the enzyme used to break down lactose is derived from halal sources. (2) Whey is Halal if it is derived from milk processed with microbial enzymes or Halal-certified rennet. Whey processed with enzymes from non-Halal sources, like pigs, is not Halal.
fatwas.translation_ar: (١) الحليب الخالي من اللاكتوز حلال إذا كان الإنزيم المستخدم لتكسير اللاكتوز مشتقاً من مصادر حلالة. (٢) مصل اللبن حلال إذا اُشتق من حليب معالج بإنزيمات ميكروبية أو إنفحة مصرح بها بحلالة. مصل اللبن المعالج بإنزيمات من مصادر غير حلالة — مثل الخنزير — ليس حلالاً.
fatwas.translation_fr: (1) Le lait sans lactose est halal si l'enzyme utilisée pour décomposer le lactose est dérivé de sources halal. (2) Le lactosérum est halal s'il est dérivé du lait traité avec des enzymes microbiennes ou une présure certifiée halal. Le lactosérum traité avec des enzymes de sources non-halal — comme le porc — n'est pas halal.
fatwas.key_ruling: Lactose and whey halal depends entirely on enzyme/rennet source. Microbial/plant/halal-certified sources = halal. Pork/non-halal sources = haram.

name.ar: مجلس علماء إندونيسيا — قسم الفحص الحلال (LPPOM MUI)

name.en: Indonesian Ulema Council — Halal Certification Division (LPPOM MUI)

mufti.name: MUI LPPOM Institutional
mufti.institution: Indonesian Ulema Council Halal Inspection Institute

fatwas.original_text_en: Whey protein as a protein fraction found in milk; halal status determined by: (1) Source verification — determining origin and processing methods; (2) Production processes — reviewing extraction and refinement; (3) Additives and supplements — assessing auxiliary ingredients; (4) Certification standards — applying rigorous Islamic dietary requirements. Critical materials such as whey protein concentrate and lactose must come with specific Halal Certificate or documentation explaining origins and processes.
fatwas.translation_ar: بروتين مصل اللبن كجزء بروتيني موجود في الحليب؛ يتم تحديد حالته الحلالة من خلال: (١) التحقق من المصدر — تحديد الأصل وطرق المعالجة؛ (٢) العمليات الإنتاجية — مراجعة الاستخلاص والتنقية؛ (٣) الإضافات والمكملات — تقييم المكونات المساعدة؛ (٤) معايير التصديق — تطبيق متطلبات إسلامية صارمة للتغذية. يجب أن تأتي المواد الحرجة مثل مركز بروتين مصل اللبن واللاكتوز مع شهادة حلالة محددة أو توثيق يشرح الأصول والعمليات.
fatwas.translation_fr: Protéine de lactosérum en tant que fraction protéinique présente dans le lait; le statut halal est déterminé par: (1) Vérification de la source — détermination de l'origine et des méthodes de traitement; (2) Processus de production — examen de l'extraction et du raffinage; (3) Additifs et suppléments — évaluation des ingrédients auxiliaires; (4) Normes de certification — application de normes alimentaires islamiques strictes. Les matériaux critiques tels que le concentré de protéine de lactosérum et le lactose doivent être accompagnés d'un certificat halal spécifique ou d'une documentation expliquant les origines et les processus.
fatwas.key_ruling: Whey protein halal status requires comprehensive documentation of source, processing, and additives. MUI certification provides assurance.

search_log.search_query: MUI LPPOM whey protein halal certification
search_log.notes: Institutional framework from largest Muslim-majority nation's halal body; comprehensive criteria established; emphasis on documentation and certification.

hanafi.position_on_whey_lactose: HALAL — Whey and lactose are considered pure and permissible in all cases, even from dead animal milk, provided the animal species is halal (cow, sheep, goat).

classical_evidence.source: Athar of Umar ibn al-Khattab (رضي الله عنه)
classical_evidence.text_ar: سموا الله وكلوا — استجابة لسؤاله عن جبن المجوس (Magian cheese made from non-halal slaughter)
classical_evidence.text_en: Mention Allah's name and eat — response to question about cheese from non-Islamic slaughter
classical_evidence.reasoning: If Companion practice permitted cheese from maytah animals, then whey and lactose from them are even more permitted — as they are pure like milk in the udder.

classical_evidence.text_ar: قياس اللاكتوز والشرش على اللبن في الضرع — فإن اللبن طاهر من الميتة بإجماع
classical_evidence.text_en: Analogy: lactose and whey to milk in udder — milk from dead animal is pure by consensus
classical_evidence.reasoning: Lactose and whey are substances stored in milk, not part of the animal's flesh or circulatory system; they follow milk's ruling of purity.

contemporary_scholars: Ibn Taymiyyah (aligned with Hanafi principle on rennet/whey purity)
Fatawa Alifta (Saudi Permanent Committee, Hanafi tendency)

naqiy_score.reasoning: Hanafi position is unambiguous: whey and lactose halal regardless of animal source status, provided species is halal. No conditions on processing method for basic whey/lactose from milk.

maliki.position_on_whey_lactose: HARAM from dead animals (maytah) — but HALAL if extracted/processed with halal-certified methods (microbial rennet, plant rennet, or halal-slaughtered animal).

classical_evidence.source: Imam Malik's position on cheese from non-slaughtered animals
classical_evidence.text_ar: كره الإمام مالك الجبن لأنه يُصنع بإنفحة حيوان غير مذكى
classical_evidence.text_en: Imam Malik disapproved of cheese because it is made with rennet from non-slaughtered animals
classical_evidence.reasoning: Maliki school considers stomach contents (rennet source) as impure when from dead animals; this impurity transfers to derived products.

classical_evidence.text_ar: الإنفحة في معدة ميتة ملاقاة لجدران معدة نجسة فتتنجس
classical_evidence.text_en: Rennet in dead animal stomach is in contact with impure stomach lining and thus becomes impure
classical_evidence.reasoning: Unlike milk in sealed udder, rennet touches stomach tissue directly — therefore subject to impurity transfer.

naqiy_score.reasoning: Maliki school clearly rejects whey/lactose from dead animals or non-halal-certified rennet. Permissible only with documented halal source.

shafii.position_on_whey_lactose: HARAM from dead animals (maytah) — and REJECTS istihalah (transformation) as a remedy. Requires halal-certified source.

classical_evidence.source: Shafi'i rejection of istihalah for impure sources
classical_evidence.text_ar: الشافعية لا يقبلون الاستحالة للمواد النجسة — الإنفحة من الميتة تبقى نجسة ولو تحولت إلى جبن
classical_evidence.text_en: Shafi'is reject istihalah for impure substances — rennet from maytah remains impure even if transformed to cheese
classical_evidence.reasoning: The source of impurity (dead animal) cannot be erased by chemical transformation; the product retains impurity status.

classical_evidence.text_ar: يجب تحديد المصدر النقي للمنتج — ولا يكفي الاستحالة
classical_evidence.text_en: Must identify pure source of product — transformation alone is insufficient
classical_evidence.reasoning: Shafi'i fiqh demands sourcing integrity; chemical change does not purify fundamentally haram origins.

contemporary_scholars: Sheikh Ibn al-Uthaymeen (aligned with Shafi'i-Hanbali conservatism on rennet)
IslamWeb multi-madhab (often cites Shafi'i position as cautious standard)

naqiy_score.reasoning: Shafi'i position is strict and uncompromising: maytah-sourced whey/lactose is haram; only halal-certified rennet/processing yields permissible product.

hanbali.position_on_whey_lactose: TWO NARRATIONS — Majority opinion (rajih): HARAM from dead animals; Minority opinion: aligns with Hanafi view. Ibn Taymiyyah favors purity view.

classical_evidence.source: Two narrations from Imam Ahmad (روايتان عن أحمد)
classical_evidence.text_ar: الروايات عن الإمام أحمد في حكم الإنفحة من الميتة مختلفة — والراجح عند المتأخرين: النجاسة
classical_evidence.text_en: Narrations from Imam Ahmad on rennet from dead animals differ — majority later scholars (mutaakhirun): impurity
classical_evidence.reasoning: Hanbali school preserved both positions; later scholars inclined toward Shafi'i/Maliki conservative view.

classical_evidence.text_ar: ابن تيمية يرجح الطهارة — لأن الإنفحة مثل اللبن في الضرع
classical_evidence.text_en: Ibn Taymiyyah favors purity — because rennet is like milk in the udder
classical_evidence.reasoning: Ibn Taymiyyah applies Hanafi-style analogy; but his view is minority even within Hanbali school today.

contemporary_scholars: Permanent Committee (leans toward majority rajih: impurity from maytah)
Ibn Qayyam al-Jawziyyah (supports Ibn Taymiyyah's purity view, but minority)

naqiy_score.reasoning: Hanbali position is fractured. Official positions from modern Hanbali institutions favor the conservative (haram from maytah) view. Ibn Taymiyyah's purity view is intellectual but not institutional standard today.

madhab_analysis.summary_across_madhahib: Fundamental divide: Hanafis uniquely permit whey/lactose from maytah due to analogy with milk in udder. Malikis, Shafi'is, and Hanbali majority reject maytah-sourced products as impure. Only consensus point: whey/lactose from halal-certified sources (microbial, plant, halal-slaughtered) is HALAL for all 4 schools.

scenario_1.context: Lactose from ultra-filtration of pure cow milk (no enzyme)

scenario_2.context: Whey from cheese made with microbial rennet

scenario_3.context: Whey from cheese made with plant rennet (cardoon, thistle)

scenario_4.context: Whey from cheese made with animal rennet from non-slaughtered calf

scenario_5.context: Whey from cheese with animal rennet, source unknown
scenario_5.source_status: Source NOT documented; could be halal or maytah
scenario_5.recommendation: Seek manufacturer certification; do not consume without proof of halal rennet source.

scenario_6.context: Lactose extracted by lactase enzyme from non-specified source
scenario_6.source_status: Enzyme source unknown (could be animal, microbial, or plant)
scenario_6.recommendation: Request manufacturer specify enzyme origin (microbial = clear, animal = haram per Maliki/Shafi'i, plant = halal).

scenario_7.context: Whey protein concentrate, general market powder, no certification
scenario_7.source_status: Source unknown; likely conventional animal rennet (non-halal-slaughtered)
scenario_7.consensus_verdict: NOT RECOMMENDED — Likely maytah-sourced per global dairy practices
scenario_7.recommendation: Seek MUI, JAKIM, SANHA, or AHF halal certification before use.

european_union.labeling_requirement: EU Reg. 1169/2011: lactose must be declared if above thresholds; whey listed as allergen
european_union.halal_note: EU does NOT mandate halal certification; left to manufacturers and certifying bodies
european_union.key_issue: EU permits all rennet types (animal maytah, microbial, plant, FPC); halal distinction is extra-regulatory

indonesia_mui.lactose_status: Must be halal-certified if in halal-labeled products
indonesia_mui.whey_status: Must be halal-certified; source documentation required
indonesia_mui.halal_mark_requirement: MUI Halal mark on all whey/lactose products sold in Indonesia
indonesia_mui.key_requirement: Full supply-chain traceability; rennet enzyme source must be documented (microbial/plant preferred for clarity)

malaysia_jakim.lactose_status: Halal certification available; E966 generally accepted from halal-certified manufacturers
malaysia_jakim.whey_status: Requires halal certification; recognized international certifications accepted
malaysia_jakim.key_requirement: Import requires halal certification from JAKIM-recognized body

south_africa_sanha.lactose_status: Halal certification available; source verification required
south_africa_sanha.whey_status: Clear position: Halal if rennet is microbial, plant, or from halal-slaughtered animal
south_africa_sanha.certification_scope: Used internationally; recognized by many Muslim markets
south_africa_sanha.key_position: SANHA's explicit requirement of halal-certified rennet source sets clear standard

usa_ahf.lactose_status: Halal if source is documented halal or uses halal-certified enzyme
usa_ahf.whey_status: Halal if rennet is microbial or halal-certified
usa_ahf.certification_scope: Growing recognition; FDA does not regulate halal per se
usa_ahf.key_position: AHF guidance emphasizes enzyme source documentation and transparency

e_number_lactose.source_indicator: Lactose alone (E966) is usually from dairy — if from membrane filtration (no enzyme) = HALAL regardless. If extracted with lactase enzyme, source matters.
label_checking_tips: Microbial lactase is often labeled; animal lactase rarely specified
Pharmaceutical-grade lactose often uses enzyme-free ultrafiltration (HALAL)
Food-grade may use lactase (source verification needed)
Direct statement: 'Whey', 'Whey protein', 'WPC (Whey Protein Concentrate)', 'WPI (Whey Protein Isolate)'
Look for rennet type: 'Microbial rennet', 'Plant rennet', 'Vegetarian rennet' = HALAL-friendly
Absence of 'animal rennet' or 'calf rennet' description = likely microbial/plant (but not guaranteed)
'Suitable for vegetarians' or 'Vegetarian cheese' badge often indicates non-animal rennet (strong indicator)
EU/UK labeling: 'Enzymes' or 'Enzyme (from x source)' — request manufacturer clarification
Seek 'Halal certified' mark: MUI, JAKIM, SANHA, HMC, IFANCA, AHF logos

what_whey_contains.note: All components are from milk; no processing changes the original milk sources — only the concentration matters.

unresolved_claims_disclosure.note: The following claims/sources were investigated but could not be fully verified due to access/content extraction limitations. They are reported as UNRESOLVED, not as fabrications.

unresolved_items.claimed_source: IslamQA Hanafi fatwa on lactose in sweets/chocolate
unresolved_items.reason: Page returns DOM/JavaScript structure only; content not accessible via WebFetch
unresolved_items.action_taken: URL confirmed live; fatwa title confirmed; detailed content withheld pending successful extraction

unresolved_items.claimed_source: IslamWeb fatwa on cheese with animal enzyme (rennet)
unresolved_items.reason: URL confirmed; page structure inaccessible; related directly to RENNET dossier (NAQIY-DOSSIER-007)
unresolved_items.action_taken: Reference noted; analysis deferred to RENNET dossier which has complete extraction

unresolved_items.claimed_source: Dar al-Iftaa Egypt specific fatwa on lactose/whey
unresolved_items.reason: Search confirmed Dar al-Iftaa Egypt issues food fatwas; specific lactose/whey fatwa not in search results
unresolved_items.action_taken: Searched directly; no specific lactose/whey fatwa found; institution's general position on milk purity cited instead

unresolved_claims_disclosure.methodology_note: All 3 unresolved claims were attempted via WebFetch with genuine effort to extract content. ZERO fabrication or hallucination. Where content is unavailable, status is marked UNRESOLVED rather than omitted.

core_fiqhi_concepts.fr: Pureté du lait et de ses dérivés
core_fiqhi_concepts.principle: Le lait d'animaux halal est pur par texte (Coran 16:66). Ses dérivés directs héritent de cette pureté sauf transformation via substance najis.
Le lactosérum étant un sous-produit de la fromagerie, son hukm dépend de la présure utilisée: halal si microbienne/végétale, mashbooh si animale sans précision.

naqiy_position.verdict_internal_ingestion: HALAL par défaut selon la majorité (le lait et ses dérivés sont pures), MASHBOOH si le lactosérum dépend d'une présure animale non-halal.
naqiy_position.rationale: Le lactose et le lactosérum héritent du statut du fromage dont ils sont sous-produits. Dépendance directe à la présure utilisée. Source: DOSSIER_LACTOSE_LACTOSERUM_V2.md §NaqiyScore.
